sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 2-hour history of worsening low back pain and tingling sensation in the right foot, which began after raking leaves in the yard.
He reports that the pain is exacerbated by standing and improves with rest and a hot shower.
He denies taking any medication for the pain prior to presentation.
He has a past history of similar back pain 3 years ago that resolved within a day.
His partner reports similar symptoms, with pain upon standing but not bending over.
The patient's partner also reports that the patient's pain tolerance is high, and he would not typically report pain unless it was significant.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in the right leg.
Current medications: None mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has some pain to palpation around the L5 region.
He has normal dorsiflexion and equal pulses in all extremities.
Test Results: The patient had a normal x-ray of the low back.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute low back strain, likely due to raking leaves, with radicular symptoms in the right leg.
Problem list: Low back strain; Radicular pain; [Unknown].
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg PO BID for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help improve flexibility and strength in the low back and alleviate pain.
The patient will follow up with me in two weeks to reassess symptoms and consider further imaging, such as an MRI, if the symptoms do not improve.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in two weeks to reassess symptoms.
